reasoning: Plan is well-structured and all assumptions validated against the codebase. The CPU-to-GPU migration path is sound: basePositions, hueOffsets, and all VisualParams (bassEnergy, trebleEnergy, motionAmplitude, etc.) are confirmed available. ShaderMaterial drop-in for PointsMaterial is straightforward with Three.js 0.183.2. Layered-sine noise avoids external dependencies while meeting the time-evolution criterion. Synthetic bass fallback in renderLoop.ts already ensures visuals continue without audio. The 20 tests cover all acceptance criteria and edge cases.
warnings: pointerX/pointerY live on FrameState directly (frame.pointerX, frame.pointerY), not inside frame.params — draw() must read them from the correct location when updating uPointerPos

Existing tests in pointCloud.test.ts assert CPU-side position buffer changes (e.g., 'positions change between frames with non-zero bassEnergy') — these will need rewriting since GPU-side deformation means posAttr.needsUpdate will no longer be set and position buffers won't change on CPU side

The ?raw GLSL import needs a type declaration added to src/vite-env.d.ts (which already exists with Vite client types) — step 5 correctly identifies this but should target the existing file rather than creating a new src/shaders.d.ts
